title: Sensitivity Labels in Microsoft Fabric
summary: Sensitivity labels from Microsoft Purview Information Protection are now returned inline by the Fabric Items API. Use them for governance automation, AI agent guardrails, and compliance inventory.

how_labels_work.source: Labels are defined in Microsoft Purview (requires M365 E5 licensing) and published via label policies to users/groups.
how_labels_work.api_behavior: The sensitivityLabel field (containing the label UUID) is returned on items that have a label assigned. Items without labels omit the field entirely.
how_labels_work.no_include_needed: Unlike other optional fields (e.g., DefaultIdentity), sensitivity labels are returned by default — no --include parameter needed.
how_labels_work.only_uuid_returned: The Fabric API returns only the label UUID (not the name). Label definitions (names, descriptions, classification levels) are managed in Purview and queryable only via Microsoft Graph.
how_labels_work.cannot_update_existing: The PATCH /items/{id} endpoint does NOT support changing sensitivity labels. Labels can only be set at creation time (workspace-scoped) or via admin bulk operations.

resolving_label_names.why_needed: The Fabric API only returns label UUIDs. To make classification decisions (e.g., block access to Highly Confidential items), agents need the UUID-to-name mapping.
resolving_label_names.permission_required: InformationProtection.Read (comes with M365 E5 user, no Fabric admin role needed)
resolving_label_names.caching_advice: Labels change infrequently. Cache the mapping and refresh periodically (e.g., daily) rather than querying Graph on every invocation.

reading_labels.type_specific: fabio lakehouse list --workspace $WS (shows SENSITIVITY LABEL column in table output when labels are present)

writing_labels.all_types_supported: Every type-specific create command (50 total) accepts --sensitivity-label.
writing_labels.bulk_rate_limit: Maximum 25 bulk requests per hour, up to 2000 items per request.
writing_labels.assignment_method: Standard (automated, default) or Priviledged (manual by admin). Note: API misspells 'Privileged' as 'Priviledged'.

ai_agent_guardrail_workflow.step_3_classify: Cross-reference the label UUID with the cached map to determine classification level (Public, Internal, Confidential, Highly Confidential)
ai_agent_guardrail_workflow.step_4_decide: Block writes to Highly Confidential items, require approval for Confidential, allow Public/Internal freely

governance_patterns: Scan all workspaces for unlabeled items and generate a compliance report
Enforce mandatory labeling by checking newly created items periodically
Use bulk-set-labels to remediate unlabeled items programmatically
Combine with item tags for multi-dimensional classification
Auto-label new items based on workspace naming conventions or content analysis
Alert when high-sensitivity items are created in non-production workspaces

prerequisites.reading_labels_on_items: Any workspace viewer role — labels are in the standard item response.
prerequisites.resolving_label_names: InformationProtection.Read permission on Microsoft Graph (M365 E5 user).
prerequisites.setting_labels_on_create: Workspace ReadWrite role + Purview label policy published to the user.
prerequisites.admin_bulk_operations: Fabric Administrator role. Service principals NOT supported — user principal only.

common_errors.label_not_found: The specified label UUID does not exist or is not published to the calling user. Verify the label ID in Purview compliance portal.
common_errors.insufficient_license: M365 E5 or equivalent licensing is required for sensitivity label operations.
common_errors.no_purview: Sensitivity label features require Microsoft Purview Information Protection to be configured in the tenant.
common_errors.cannot_update_label: PATCH endpoint does not support label changes. To change a label on an existing item: use admin bulk-set-labels (requires admin role) or delete and recreate the item.
common_errors.service_principal_not_supported: Bulk label operations only support user principals, not service principals or managed identities.
